subject: [SDNC] Set STDOUT Log level
commit_message:
[SDNC] Set STDOUT Log level

Use of common.log.level template in order to set the log level for
STDOUT on SDNC.

Issue-ID: OOM-2516
Signed-off-by: Sylvain Desbureaux <sylvain.desbureaux@orange.com>
Change-Id: I1fde92c11f769a028292325bf67ed9654948a46f

Patch Set 2:

Based on https://gating-results.onap.eu/results//110437-2/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s/pod-onap-sdnc-0-sdnc-logs.html I would say it is working now.

I noticed a few plugins have quite noisy startup (printing whole stack track during warning). If I find time I would clean that up.

The console appender is using the pattern defined at
https://gerrit.onap.org/r/gitweb?p=oom.git;a=blob;f=kubernetes/sdnc/resources/config/log/org.ops4j.pax.logging.cfg;hb=HEAD#l50

We could attempt switching to JSON or XML if desired.

One thing to consider is right now each different type of log has its own pattern. By type of log I mean audit vs metric vs error.

Right now metric and audit would not be getting printed on the console (stdout) at all right now. Right now those are not going to the root logger and the console appender is only getting log statements from the root logger.

Yes I agree, cool! thanks

OK, nice.

>

we can try on this one yes, I've seen the pattern but wasn't sure on how to change it

> 
> One thing to consider is right now each different type of log has its own pattern. By type of log I mean audit vs metric vs error.
> 
> Right now metric and audit would not be getting printed on the console (stdout) at all right now. Right now those are not going to the root logger and the console appender is only getting log statements from the root logger.

So If I understand well, "debug.log", "error.log", "audit.log" have a different pattern in the file (as we can see in org.ops4j.pax.logging.cfg)
And on STDOUT, we'll see only logs with INFO and up (per default) with another specific pattern. And only if they are sent to the root logger (which is not the case for audit and metric).

Is it right?

I believe we can stick with that for now and work on the "best" way to generate the logs.
So let's try JSON or XML (preference to JSON personnally) and see what we have!

thanks again

Replying in a separate message

Patch Set 2:

we can try on this one yes, I've seen the pattern but wasn't sure on how to change it

Updating the pattern is very easy, patterns are only relevant if using a PatternLayout. I used one which was already in the file, but if we want something custom I can change it.

If we switch to JSON or XML that is a different kind of layout. It comes with some default formatting. You can provide custom formatting, but it isn't through patterns, but a custom formatter class.

So If I understand well, "debug.log", "error.log", "audit.log" have a different pattern in the file (as we can see in org.ops4j.pax.logging.cfg)
Correct.

And on STDOUT, we'll see only logs with INFO and up (per default) with another specific pattern. 
Not sure if saying "and up" is the right way. Trace has the integer logging level of 600.
Error has the integer logging level of 200.
If logging level is set to INFO you will get all logs for INFO and anything more severe (lower logging level).

So with INFO you get INFO, WARN, ERROR, FATAL.

https://logging.apache.org/log4j/2.x/manual/customloglevels.html for greater detail.

And only if they are sent to the root logger (which is not the case for audit and metric).
Correct. 

I can check the loggers for audit and metric to start sending records to the ConsoleAppender if desired.

I believe we could define multiple console appenders each with a different pattern layout.

OK understood!
pattern is for "text output" and switch for JSON or XML is "structured output" (sorry if the name are wrong)

Can we try the default formatting on JSON for STDOUT?

for the metric / audit part, according to what I understand of https://docs.onap.org/en/elalto/submodules/logging-analytics.git/docs/Logging_Enhancements_Project/logging_enhancements_project.html#types-of-eelf-logs, audit should be superseded by metric, so maybe adding this one to STDOUT could be interesting!

> OK understood!
> pattern is for "text output" and switch for JSON or XML is "structured output" (sorry if the name are wrong)
> 
> Can we try the default formatting on JSON for STDOUT?
> 
> for the metric / audit part, according to what I understand of https://docs.onap.org/en/elalto/submodules/logging-analytics.git/docs/Logging_Enhancements_Project/logging_enhancements_project.html#types-of-eelf-logs, audit should be superseded by metric, so maybe adding this one to STDOUT could be interesting!

I tried XML and JSON, but ran into issues. I added details on the JIRA ticket associated with this change set.

I think we can create multiple appenders all writing to stdout using different patterns. Then audit and metric could go to stdout. Should we try that?
